advanced.title: Auto-pick weights
advanced.description: How the auto-pick understands your watch habits. Recent rows count more than old ones; total minutes watched matters more than total episodes.
advanced.unavailable: Could not load weights right now.
advanced.lockInactiveDescription: Total weighted history {current} is below the floor {floor}. Watch a bit more and the auto-pick will start learning.
advanced.coarseDescription: Drives the lock decision (which language and dub-vs-sub).
advanced.fineDescription: Picks the team within the lock. Showing top 8 by weight.
advanced.resetDescription: Wipes per-anime locks. Your watch history is kept, so the auto-pick will relearn from scratch.
advanced.resetConfirm: Reset all per-anime preferences? Your watch history will be kept, but the auto-pick will start fresh.
advanced.resetSuccess: Preferences reset. The next anime you open will use community popularity until weights rebuild.

recs.title: Recs Debug
recs.pickerHelp: Enter a username, public ID, or UUID to view that user's rec breakdown.
recs.tableCaption: Recommendation candidates for user, ranked by final score
recs.s1Desc: Predicted score via Pearson-correlated taste neighbours (k-NN over the user's anime_list scores). Cold-start: needs enough rated anime, otherwise contributes 0.
recs.s2Desc: Max Jaccard overlap between the candidate's genres and the user's top-rated seed anime.
recs.s3Desc: Distinct users who started watching the anime in the last 30 days (population trending).
recs.s4Desc: Pure metadata freshness: ongoing, or aired within the last 90 days.
recs.s5Desc: Time-weighted TF-IDF affinity over tags (0.30), studios (0.25), genres (0.15), demographic (0.10), source (0.10), kind (0.10) from watch history.
recs.s6Desc: Post-rank pin at #1: "Because you finished X". Cascade: local co-occurrence → Shikimori /similar → score-5 fallback. Fires on a completed anime scored ≥7.
recs.s7Desc: Negative signal: max genre+tag Jaccard vs anime the user DROPPED (drops scored ≥7 excluded; needs ≥2 dropped seeds). Negative weight demotes look-alikes.
recs.s11Desc: Not a score — removes anime already on the user's list (completed / dropped / hidden) from the candidate pool. See the filter audit panel below.
recs.s12Desc: Post-rank greedy MMR re-rank (λ=0.3, similarity over genres+studios, max 3 items with an identical genre set). The arrow in the Rank column shows how far S12 moved a row.

collections.title: Collections (admin)
collections.deleteConfirm: Delete this collection? Items are unlinked but anime records remain.
collections.itemsEmpty: No anime yet — add one below.

q1.a: AnimeEnigma is a self-hosted anime streaming platform with Shikimori and MAL integration. It combines metadata from Shikimori with video from multiple sources, plus tools like recommendations, OP/ED ratings, watch parties, and personal lists — all in one place.

q2.q: Is it free? Are there ads?
q2.a: Yes, free and ad-free. The platform is self-hosted by a small group, not a commercial service. There's no advertising, no tracking pixels, and no subscription tier — everyone gets the same access.

q3.q: Where do the videos come from?
q3.a: AnimeEnigma streams videos from Kodik and AnimeLib (Russian audio/subs). The backend proxies the streams so your IP never hits the external CDN directly. English-source players are being rebuilt — new providers are coming.

q4.a: We combine five signals: top-list similarity (S1), genre affinity (S2), global trending (S3), rating boost (S4), and seasonal freshness (S5). Each signal contributes a weighted score and the final list is mixed. No personal data leaves the server — recommendations are computed locally from your activity.

q5.q: Can I import my MAL list?
q5.a: Yes. Go to Profile → Settings → MAL Import and enter your MAL username. The importer pulls your full list (statuses, scores, episodes watched, dates) and resolves each title to the AnimeEnigma catalog. Re-running the import updates entries that changed on MAL.

q6.a: Not yet — the web app is fully mobile-responsive and works offline-ish via the browser cache. A native app may come later, but for now installing the site as a PWA (Add to Home Screen) is the closest thing.

q7.q: How do I report a bug or request a feature?
q7.a: Use the "Send feedback" link in the page footer — pick Bug, Issue, or Feature, optionally describe it, and submit. The report is saved on the server and an admin gets a Telegram notification. Console logs and network requests are auto-attached to help debugging.

q8.a: A small self-hosted group of anime fans. The code is open and contributions are welcome via the project repository. No company, no shareholders, no roadmap pressure — just folks who wanted a better way to watch anime.
